tasks.description: Write updated landing page hero copy — x402-native positioning vs Stripe MPP
tasks.context:
CRITICAL OUTPUT FORMAT: Write plain Markdown directly to the output file. Do NOT wrap content in code fences or backtick blocks. The file must start with a # heading or plain text, not with backticks. Write updated landing page hero copy for invoica.ai reflecting the Stripe MPP competitive moment.

Context:
- Stripe Multi-Party Payments + Visa Agentic Ready launched March 17-18, 2026
- These are Web2 solutions retrofitted for AI agents — they require Stripe accounts, USD rails, KYC
- Invoica = x402-native from day 1: stablecoin, permissionless, multi-chain, open protocol
- Positioning: "Invoica = x402 ecosystem's enterprise compliance without platform lock-in"
- Key differentiators: open x402 vs walled garden, $0.0001 vs $0.30+ per transaction, no Stripe account needed, multi-chain vs Ethereum-only

Deliverable: a Markdown file containing the new copy, NOT code changes (human will implement)

Write:
1. Hero headline (max 8 words)
2. Hero subheadline (max 20 words)  
3. 3-bullet value prop (≤10 words each)
4. CTA button text (2-4 words)
5. "Why not Stripe MPP?" section (3 comparison rows: Invoica vs Stripe MPP)
6. Alternative headline options (3 variants)

Tone: direct, technical, confident — not anti-Stripe, just x402-native.

Output file: reports/positioning/landing-page-copy-v2.md

review.summary: Content written directly by planner after 2x MiniMax failures (code fence wrapping loop). All spec requirements met: headline, subheadline, 3-bullet value prop, CTA, comparison table, 3 alternative headlines.
strengths: Correct tone: direct, technical, not anti-Stripe
x402-native vs Web2 retrofit framing lands clearly
Plain Markdown with no code fence wrapping

tasks.description: Write 'Invoica vs Stripe MPP' technical comparison document
tasks.context:
CRITICAL OUTPUT FORMAT: Write plain Markdown directly to the output file. Do NOT wrap content in code fences or backtick blocks. The file must start with a # heading or plain text, not with backticks. Write a technical comparison document: Invoica vs Stripe Multi-Party Payments (and Visa Agentic Ready).

This doc will be:
- Published on invoica.ai/blog or docs
- Shared with prospects who ask "why not just use Stripe?"
- Referenced in sales calls

Invoica facts (all verified, shipped):
- x402 protocol: open standard, no Stripe account needed
- Stablecoin payments: USDC on Base, Solana, Polygon
- Transaction cost: ~$0.0001 (gas) vs Stripe's 2.9% + $0.30
- Multi-chain: Base, Solana, Polygon
- Tax compliance: 12 countries built-in
- SAP B1 ERP integration: live
- Settlement detection: on-chain, real-time
- No KYC required for agent-to-agent payments
- Open protocol: any agent stack (LangChain, AutoGPT, custom)

Stripe MPP facts (from public announcement March 17, 2026):
- Requires Stripe account and KYC
- USD/fiat rails primarily
- Centralized settlement
- Agentic payments = Stripe's payment intent flow adapted for AI
- Higher cost structure

Format:
- Title: "Invoica vs Stripe MPP: x402-native vs Web2 retrofit"
- Comparison table: 8 rows, 3 columns (Feature | Invoica | Stripe MPP)
- Narrative paragraphs: problem, approach, when to use each
- Length: ~600 words + table
- Tone: neutral, technical, no trash-talking Stripe

Output file: reports/positioning/invoica-vs-stripe-mpp.md

review.summary: Content written directly by planner after 2x MiniMax failures. 8-row comparison table, ~650 word narrative, correct title, plain Markdown format.
strengths: Clear 'when to use each' guidance for prospects
Technical accuracy verified against shipped features only

tasks.description: Write Circle Nanopayments integration feasibility brief
tasks.context:
CRITICAL OUTPUT FORMAT: Write plain Markdown directly to the output file. Do NOT wrap content in code fences or backtick blocks. The file must start with a # heading or plain text, not with backticks. Write a brief technical feasibility assessment: should Invoica integrate Circle Nanopayments?

Context:
- Circle Nanopayments: gas-free USDC settlements as low as $0.000001 (testnet)
- Enables sub-cent x402 invoicing — a major unlock for high-frequency agent micropayments
- CEO flagged as strategic research priority
- Question: can Invoica's settlement detection + tax engine work with nanopayment transaction formats?

Write:
1. What Circle Nanopayments is (2 paragraphs, technical)
2. How it would work with Invoica's stack (settlement detection layer, tax implications for sub-cent transactions)
3. Integration complexity: Low / Medium / High + why
4. Recommended action: build now / wait for mainnet / skip
5. Potential competitive advantage if shipped

Length: ~400 words
Format: technical brief (not a blog post)

Output file: reports/research/circle-nanopayments-feasibility.md

review.summary: Content written directly by planner after 2x MiniMax failures. ~420 words, technical brief format, Medium complexity rating, recommended action: wait for mainnet.
strengths: Correct technical brief format (not blog post)
Tax aggregation gap identified as pre-work action regardless of Circle timeline
